title: Wrong KV cache update for sliding-window attention layers when total sequence length reaches window size
issue_summary: Sliding-window cache maintenance dropped an element too early when the total sequence length hit the window size exactly, corrupting the boundary case.

root_cause.summary: The cache update condition rolled the window one step too early at the exact window boundary.
root_cause.basis: issue report and linked fix PR

symptom: At the first full window, the oldest token is discarded before it should be, so the cache content is wrong even before the sequence exceeds the window.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case isolates the boundary update rule and shows that the buggy branch drops the oldest token when the cache merely reaches the window size instead of exceeding it.

issue_contract.trigger: Update a sliding-window cache at the first step where the cache exactly reaches the window size.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy path rolls the cache one step too early at the exact boundary.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: The oldest token is dropped too soon and the boundary cache contents are wrong.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces the early rolling condition in sliding-window cache maintenance.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the reported symptom that the cache contents are wrong exactly at the first full-window boundary.
